steps.title: Identify the tense framework
steps.content: The sentence describes a general, timeless fact about wood frogs using present-tense verbs (“is unable,” “helps prevent”). The blank verb must maintain this present-tense framework.

steps.title: Apply the rule: use simple present for timeless facts
steps.content:
”Survives” uses simple present tense, matching the sentence's timeless-fact context about wood frog biology.

The wrong choices violate the present-tense rule by using past tense forms (“had survived,” “survived”) or conditional (“would survive”), which would shift the statement to describe a specific past event or hypothetical situation rather than a general truth.

This matches the correct answer — D is correct.
